title: SolarWinds Orion Supply Chain Attack
summary: Nation-state actor (SUNBURST/Cozy Bear) inserted a backdoor into SolarWinds Orion software update. The malicious update was distributed to 18,000+ organizations including US government agencies. Dwell time exceeded 9 months before detection. The attacker moved laterally into email systems and stole sensitive data.

title: Okta 2022 Support System Breach (LAPSUS$)
summary: LAPSUS$ threat group compromised a third-party support engineer's laptop at Sitel, which had administrative access to Okta's customer support tools. Screenshots showed access to customer tenants. Okta delayed disclosure by months. As an identity provider, the breach had cascading risk for downstream customers relying on Okta for SSO.

title: LastPass 2022 Developer Environment Breach
summary: Attackers compromised a LastPass developer's home computer via a vulnerable media software package, stealing credentials to access LastPass cloud storage. Customer password vault backups (encrypted) were exfiltrated along with unencrypted metadata. Millions of users were potentially affected with their encrypted vaults exposed.

title: CircleCI 2023 Security Incident
summary: An attacker compromised a CircleCI employee laptop with malware, stealing a valid 2FA-backed SSO session cookie. This granted access to production systems where customer environment variables, tokens, and keys stored in CircleCI projects were exfiltrated. CircleCI urged all customers to immediately rotate all secrets.

title: Twilio 2022 Phishing Attack
summary: Attackers sent SMS phishing messages to Twilio employees impersonating IT, stealing credentials and bypassing 2FA. Attackers accessed customer data including phone numbers and Authy 2FA accounts. The breach was part of a coordinated campaign (0ktapus) targeting 130+ organizations. Customer account data and verification codes were accessed.

title: Uber 2022 Internal Network Compromise
summary: An 18-year-old attacker used MFA fatigue attack on an Uber contractor, then found admin credentials in a PowerShell script on an internal network share. The attacker gained access to Uber's internal tools including AWS, GCP, Slack, HackerOne bug reports, and internal dashboards. Source code and vulnerability reports were potentially accessed.

title: Colonial Pipeline 2021 Ransomware Attack
summary: DarkSide ransomware gang used a compromised VPN password (no MFA) to access Colonial Pipeline's network. Ransomware was deployed causing a 6-day shutdown of the largest US fuel pipeline, triggering fuel shortages across the East Coast. Colonial paid $4.4M ransom. The leaked credential had been found in a prior data breach.

title: T-Mobile 2021 Data Breach
summary: Attacker breached T-Mobile through an exposed testing environment and brute-forced API access. Over 53 million current and former customers had data exposed including SSNs, driver's license numbers, IMEI numbers, and account credentials. The attacker had access for weeks before detection. T-Mobile paid $350M in class action settlement.

title: Kaseya VSA Supply Chain Ransomware
summary: REvil ransomware gang exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Kaseya VSA, a remote management tool used by managed service providers. The attack cascaded to 60+ MSPs and 1,500+ downstream businesses. Attackers demanded $70M ransom. The vulnerability was an authentication bypass with SQL injection in the VSA web interface.

title: Microsoft Exchange ProxyLogon Exploitation
summary: Chinese APT HAFNIUM exploited four zero-day vulnerabilities (ProxyLogon) in on-premise Microsoft Exchange Server. Attackers could authenticate as any user and execute remote code. Over 250,000 servers globally were compromised before patches. Attackers installed web shells for persistent access and exfiltrated email data from government, defense, and research organizations.

title: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) Mass Exploitation
summary: Critical RCE vulnerability in Apache Log4j logging library affecting virtually all Java applications. The vulnerability (CVSS 10.0) allowed unauthenticated remote code execution via a crafted log message. Within 72 hours of disclosure, millions of exploitation attempts were recorded. Major cloud providers, enterprises, and government systems were exposed. Affected systems included iCloud, Steam, Amazon, Tesla, and countless others.

title: Equifax 2017 Data Breach
summary: Attackers exploited an unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability (CVE-2017-5638) in Equifax's online dispute portal. The breach exposed personal data of 147 million Americans including SSNs, birth dates, addresses, driver's license numbers, and ~200,000 credit card numbers. The patch was available 2 months before the breach. Equifax paid $575M+ in settlements.

title: Capital One 2019 Cloud Misconfiguration Breach
summary: A former AWS employee exploited a misconfigured web application firewall to perform SSRF, accessing AWS metadata service credentials. Using an overly permissive IAM role, they listed and downloaded data from S3 buckets containing 106 million Capital One credit card applications. Data included SSNs, bank account numbers, and credit scores.

title: Marriott/Starwood 2018 Mega Breach
summary: Nation-state attackers (linked to China's MSS) had been inside the Starwood reservation database since 2014 — 4 years before discovery. The breach was discovered post-acquisition by Marriott. 500 million guest records were compromised including passport numbers, payment card data, and travel history. The attackers maintained persistent access through Remote Access Trojans.

title: MGM Resorts 2023 Ransomware (Scattered Spider)
summary: Scattered Spider threat actors called MGM's IT help desk impersonating an employee (information gleaned from LinkedIn) to reset credentials and bypass MFA. They then deployed ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware causing a 10-day outage across MGM's casino and hotel operations, ATM failures, and slot machine shutdowns. MGM refused to pay ransom and suffered ~$100M in losses.

title: Caesars Entertainment 2023 Breach
summary: Scattered Spider used social engineering to compromise an outsourced IT vendor with access to Caesars systems. Loyalty program database was exfiltrated including SSNs and driver's license numbers for tens of millions of members. Caesars paid approximately $15M ransom (half of demanded $30M) to prevent public data release, becoming one of the first major hospitality companies to pay such a ransom.

title: MOVEit Transfer Mass Exploitation (Cl0p)
summary: Cl0p ransomware gang exploited a zero-day SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer, a widely-used managed file transfer application. Over 2,600 organizations and 77 million individuals were affected. Victims included US federal agencies, Shell, British Airways, BBC, Boots, and hundreds of others. The attackers exfiltrated data before organizations could patch the publicly disclosed vulnerability.

title: Change Healthcare 2024 Ransomware Attack
summary: ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware group compromised Change Healthcare through a Citrix portal without MFA enabled. The attack disrupted healthcare payment processing across the US, affecting prescription fulfillment, claims processing, and payments for 6 months. UnitedHealth paid $22M ransom. Over 100 million patient records were exposed — the largest US healthcare data breach. Total estimated losses exceed $3 billion.

title: Progress Software MOVEit Zero-Day (CVE-2023-34362)
summary: CVE-2023-34362: An SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer's web application allowed unauthenticated attackers to gain elevated database privileges and access arbitrary data. The vulnerability was exploited as a zero-day by Cl0p before disclosure. Attackers deployed a web shell (LEMURLOOT) to exfiltrate data. The flaw existed in multiple MOVEit Transfer versions and required no authentication to exploit.

title: Yahoo 2013-2016 Mega Breach (3 Billion Accounts)
summary: State-sponsored actors compromised Yahoo in 2013-2014, stealing data from all 3 billion user accounts — the largest breach in history. Data included names, email addresses, phone numbers, birth dates, MD5 hashed passwords, and security questions. Yahoo also forged authentication cookies. The breach was disclosed in 2016-2017 during the Verizon acquisition, reducing the acquisition price by $350M.

title: GitHub 2023 RSA SSH Key Exposure
summary: GitHub accidentally exposed its RSA SSH private key in a public GitHub repository. The key was live briefly before being rotated. Any actor who downloaded the key during the exposure window could have performed man-in-the-middle attacks on GitHub SSH connections. GitHub rotated the key within hours of discovery and confirmed no abuse, but the incident highlighted supply chain risks even at major DevOps platforms.
